content:
# Facies Analysis

**Summary**: The study of rock units that are physically and chemically similar and occur together.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] performed facies-analysis to identify five distinct lithofacies in the Lower Fadhili.

- [[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011]] performed facies analysis to identify eolian features like dunes and sand sheets.

- [[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014]] performed facies analysis to identify sedimentary environments in the Sarah Formation.

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] performed facies analysis to distinguish between fluvial, tidal, and mixed systems.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] performed facies-analysis to determine heterogeneity from north (escarpment) to south (distal).

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] performed facies-analysis to identify 15 distinct lithofacies in the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] performed facies analysis to identify four depositional sub-environments.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] performed facies-analysis to identify 13 sub-lithofacies in the Nutaysh member.

- [[2022-al-alawi|Al-Alawi, 2022]] analyzed dune and interdune facies to understand stratigraphic complexity.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] performed facies analysis to identify sedimentary lithofacies in the Al Wajh Formation.

## Scientific Context

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] Used to define the depositional environment and reservoir heterogeneity.

- [[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011]] performed facies analysis to identify eolian features like dunes and sand sheets.

- [[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014]] performed facies analysis to identify sedimentary environments in the Sarah Formation.

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] performed facies analysis to distinguish between fluvial, tidal, and mixed systems.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] performed facies-analysis to determine heterogeneity from north (escarpment) to south (distal).

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] performed facies-analysis to identify 15 distinct lithofacies in the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] performed facies analysis to identify four depositional sub-environments.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] performed facies-analysis to identify 13 sub-lithofacies in the Nutaysh member.

- [[2022-al-alawi|Al-Alawi, 2022]] analyzed dune and interdune facies to understand stratigraphic complexity.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] performed facies analysis to identify sedimentary lithofacies in the Al Wajh Formation.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] Identification of textures, fossils, and sedimentary structures.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Five facies were identified: skeletal coated-grain grainstone, peloid coated-grain grainstone/packstone, Thaumatoporella wackestone, stromatoporoid wackestone/packstone, and Pfenderina trochoidea wackestone/mudstone. ([[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010, p. 93]])

- Four distinctive aeolian depositional facies were identified: dunes, interdunes, sand sheet, and playa lakes. ([[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011, p. 10]])

- Identified lithofacies such as St, Sh, and Sp based on Miall's (1985) classification. ([[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014, p. 77]])

- Identified three main types of facies associations: fluvial-dominated I, fluvial-dominated II, and fluvial-tidal mixed. ([[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014, p. 180]])

- Identified various lithofacies including coral algal reef, mollusc-rich facies, and oolitic grainstones. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 21]])

- Fifteen lithofacies were identified and categorized into subtidal, intertidal, foreshore, supratidal, and estuarine environments. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 14]])

- Eleven lithofacies types were distinguished and listed into four groups. ([[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016, p. 122]])

- Four Pickering facies classes (A, B, C, and E) were identified based on grain size and sedimentary structures. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 198]])

- Not stated in source ([[2022-al-alawi|Al-Alawi, 2022]])

- Four main facies associations (FA1-FA4) were identified based on sedimentological features. ([[2023-butt|Butt, 2023, p. 57]])
